name: Open-label IPF D+Q pilot reported exploratory physical-function improvements
summary: The first-in-human D+Q IPF pilot supports feasibility and a human functional signal, but the result is low-confidence because it was open-label, small, disease-specific, and had unchanged pulmonary function and reported health.
statement: In 14 stable IPF participants, intermittent D+Q was feasible and associated with significant, clinically meaningful improvements in 6-minute walk distance, 4-meter gait speed, and chair-stand time, while pulmonary function, clinical chemistries, frailty index, reported health, and SASP effects were unchanged or inconclusive.
population_or_model: Participants with stable idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis
quantitative_note: The abstract reports 100% retention, planned clinical assessments completed in 13 of 14 participants, one serious adverse event, and p<0.05 improvements in the physical-function measures.
caveats: Open-label, uncontrolled design limits efficacy attribution.
The population had idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis rather than healthy aging.
SASP effects were inconclusive and pulmonary function did not improve.
